metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 batch 8 N
metadata.notes: Public-web research only; no external communication was made. Employer, breeder, applicant, licensee, agent, and legal-holder roles remain distinct. Historical rights were not assigned to later employers, and aggregated crop or owner lines are flagged for record splitting.

profiles.ownershipType: independent Japanese breeder and personal co-rightsholder with company-linked applications
profiles.ownershipSummary: MAFF records name Shinichi Kawahito personally with Shigeru and Kenichi Kawahito as holders of Tinkerberry, and with Shigeru Kawahito and Junji Tamura as holders of Sweet Charmy 2. The Chinese MIB001 grant instead names Inoue Sekkai Kogyo Co., Ltd. as rightsholder. Karin is a 2022 application jointly naming PineHillGroup Co., Ltd. and Shinichi Kawahito; it is not evidence that PineHillGroup owns his earlier varieties.
profiles.headquarters: Japan; the Karin filing places the current applicant line in Kagawa, while earlier family-held filings use Tokushima addresses
profiles.leadershipSummary: The historical bench is a family breeding group including Shigeru and Kenichi Kawahito, with Aiko and Mikiko Kawahito appearing in related records. No public succession plan or verified current operating contact was found.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Split personal/family-held varieties, Inoue Sekkai Kogyo-held MIB001, and the PineHillGroup-linked Karin application. No verified personal or business website, email, or phone was found, so no contact is supplied.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The Karin application names PineHillGroup and Shinichi Kawahito as co-applicants, but does not prove employment, equity ownership, or title to earlier Kawahito varieties.

profiles.ownershipType: former prefectural fruit-tree researcher and public-course instructor
profiles.ownershipSummary: The fruit breeder is Takahiro Tezuka of the Yamanashi Fruit Tree Experiment Station. Yamanashi Prefecture, not Tezuka personally, is the registered holder of Yume Mizuki and the institutional breeder behind the cited grape and stone-fruit portfolio.
profiles.headquarters: Yamanashi Fruit Tree Experiment Station, 1204 Ezohara, Yamanashi City, Yamanashi 405-0043, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: A 2026 prefectural syllabus identifies Tezuka as former head of the station's Environment Department and as an instructor. The station retains separate breeding, cultivation, and environment departments, providing institutional continuity after his operating role.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not confuse fruit breeder Tezuka Takahiro with the Osaka-area plant geneticist using the same Romanization but different Japanese name. This is a public-institution rollup with no personal title evidence.

profiles.ownershipType: family-company apple breeder and strategic operator without a single personal rights portfolio
profiles.ownershipSummary: Thomas Braun is the South Tyrol apple breeder associated with the Braun/KIKU platform. Fuji Fubrax was assigned to KIKU GmbH and moved to ADEMA GmbH through a 2025 company-name change. Gala Schnico Red is held by Schniga GmbH, while Golden Parsi records identify KIKU GmbH. These rights must remain separated by cultivar and assignee; ADEMA is not the owner of every Braun-bred apple.
profiles.headquarters: ADEMA GmbH, Pillhof 3, 39057 Eppan, South Tyrol, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: The family platform has visible generational continuity: Luis Braun founded Braun Nurseries, Thomas continued the breeding work, and Jürgen Braun is the current public-facing ADEMA/KIKU leader. Institutional partners and co-breeders include Egon Schmider, Andreas Gruber-Genetti, and Walter Malleier.

webResearchSources.label: Fuji Fubrax patent and KIKU-to-ADEMA name change

profiles.webResearchNotes: Split the KIKU/ADEMA, Schniga, and other assignee lines. The aggregated walnut record is not supported by the apple identity and must remain unresolved in a separate record. No trademark field is asserted because no registry record was reviewed.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: ADEMA is the documented successor name to KIKU for Fuji Fubrax and the current official portfolio platform, but no source reviewed proves Thomas Braun's current employment title or that ADEMA holds all Braun-bred varieties.

profiles.ownershipType: regional public-research technician and fruit-breeding team member
profiles.ownershipSummary: The abbreviated name resolves to Federico García Montiel, listed by IMIDA as a management technician on its fruit-improvement team. Spain's official PBR bulletin names IMIDA as applicant for IMIDA-NOVAMED-209-NCA, Alisio 30, and Levante 5; no personal rightsholder evidence was found.
profiles.parentCompany: Instituto Murciano de Investigación y Desarrollo Agrario y Medioambiental (IMIDA)
profiles.headquarters: Calle Mayor s/n, 30150 La Alberca, Murcia, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: Federico works inside a multi-person public breeding team led by José Enrique Cos Terrer, with Antonio Carrillo Navarro, Alfonso Guevara Gázquez, Lorenzo Burgos, and other named researchers. The 2023-2027 IMIDA project demonstrates funded institutional continuity.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Técnico de Gestión, IMIDA fruit breeding team
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public-institution employee profile. Preserve IMIDA as applicant/rightsholder and do not assign rights to Federico personally.

profiles.ownershipType: deceased Ohio grower, family-farm operator, and grower-association co-founder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The name resolves to John Mitchell 'Mitch' Lynd (1942-2026), co-founder of the Midwest Apple Improvement Association (MAIA). MAIA, not Lynd personally or Lynd Fruit Farm, is the documented assignee of MAIA-T and related association-bred apples.
profiles.parentCompany: Midwest Apple Improvement Association (historical co-founder affiliation)
profiles.headquarters: Newcomerstown, Ohio, United States for the MAIA rights entity; Pataskala, Ohio for the Lynd family farm
profiles.leadershipSummary: Lynd died on April 17, 2026. MAIA has a broad bench of more than 700 grower members and public leadership including President Bill Dodd, Karen Burkhart, Bill Pitts, and Hank Markgraf; the Lynd family also continues the separate farm operation.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Split the single aggregated peach record from the MAIA apple identity unless an independent source proves it belongs to this John Mitchell Lynd. This is a deceased strategic-operator profile with strong institutional succession, not a standalone personal-IP target.

profiles.ownershipType: private New Zealand trust and legal plant-rights holder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The Trustees of BMA Trust are the legal owner named for Burkitt Gala (formerly Cherry Gala) and Jive in New Zealand records and the assignee of US PP17013 for Burkitt Gala. An Australian accepted application names BMA Trust as title holder and ANFIC as agent.
profiles.headquarters: Napier, New Zealand; historical official service address c/o Mark Burkitt, 117 Gilbertson Road, R.D.3, Napier
profiles.leadershipSummary: Current trustee composition and succession are not public in the sources reviewed. Mark Burkitt/Dr Mark Burkitt is the historical service contact, while Edward Mark Burkitt is the named Burkitt Gala inventor; the sources do not establish whether those name forms refer to the same person.
profiles.primaryContactName: Australian Nurserymen's Fruit Improvement Company Ltd. (ANFIC)
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Public Australian PBR agent for BMA Trust
profiles.webResearchNotes: The trust identity and historical rights are verified, but current trustees, beneficial ownership, surviving-right status, and direct trust contact remain unresolved. ANFIC is an agent, not the owner.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Mykola Ivanovych Turovtsev was a leading cherry breeder at the Institute of Irrigated Horticulture in Melitopol. Ukraine's official variety system identifies the institute as applicant and the M.F. Sydorenko Melitopol Research Station of Horticulture within the NAAS Institute of Horticulture as owner for Krupnoplidna; this institutional title must not be converted into personal ownership.
profiles.parentCompany: Institute of Irrigated Horticulture / M.F. Sydorenko Melitopol Research Station of Horticulture, NAAS Ukraine (historical)
profiles.headquarters: Melitopol, Zaporizhzhia Oblast, Ukraine for the historical breeding program
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official institute history treats Turovtsev as a distinguished historical scientist. Valentyna Turovtseva was a major co-breeder, and later records name Anna Shkinder-Barmina, showing a multi-generation institutional bench rather than a personal succession vehicle.
profiles.primaryContactName: Institute of Horticulture, National Academy of Agrarian Sciences of Ukraine

webResearchSources.label: Ukraine official variety record for Krupnoplidna

profiles.webResearchNotes: Split the aggregated hazelnut record from this cherry breeder unless a primary record proves the identity. Old cherry records also require cultivar-by-cultivar status review because many rights are terminated or surrendered and institutional names changed over time.

profiles.ownershipType: public-interest foundation berry-breeding scientist and program leader
profiles.ownershipSummary: Lara Giongo is the Fondazione Edmund Mach berry breeder named for Abundia, Creativa, Gaia, Maia, Musa, Perla, and Janis. A 2025 government PBR bulletin names Fondazione Edmund Mach as applicant for Abundia and Creativa; no personal rightsholder evidence was found.
profiles.headquarters: Fondazione Edmund Mach, Via E. Mach 1, 38010 San Michele all'Adige, Trentino, Italy; PBR records list Giongo in nearby Lavis
profiles.leadershipSummary: Giongo has led the foundation's berry genetics and breeding work since the early 2000s and is a co-principal investigator in the VacCAP consortium. The program has a multi-researcher foundation bench and external research partners, reducing individual succession risk.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Berry genetics and breeding researcher, Fondazione Edmund Mach
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat this as a foundation-owned research portfolio. Application, grant, license, and royalty status remain jurisdiction-specific; the public email is a research-business contact and should not be interpreted as personal ownership.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Repeated foundation affiliation and applicant records are supported, but the reviewed official sources do not provide a current corporate-style job title for Giongo.

profiles.ownershipType: private Spanish plant-breeding company in a family-operated horticultural group
profiles.ownershipSummary: Masiá Breeding S.L. is a legal breeding entity formed in 2023. Canada identifies it as applicant and holder for MC18-20. Spain's 2025 bulletin records cancellation of the MC18-20 title then held by Masiá Ciscar S.A. and separately identifies Masiá Breeding as a licensee for MC16-54 and MC16-70; Spain's June 2026 bulletin identifies Masiá Breeding as the MC18-20 holder/licensor. Rights must therefore be split by jurisdiction, date, and legal entity.
profiles.headquarters: Finca Las Palmeritas, Carretera La Redondela km 1.2, 21440 Lepe, Huelva, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: The public record shows a shared-site family-group breeding operation and identifies Enrique Masiá Serrador as the MC18-20 inventor. Masiá Ciscar describes a multi-company group, but the reviewed sources do not prove the ownership percentages or management titles of Masiá Breeding.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Official public business contact at the shared Lepe site

webResearchSources.label: Spain 2025 PBR cancellation and license bulletin

profiles.webResearchNotes: Record split is mandatory: Masiá Breeding, Masiá Ciscar S.A./S.L., inventors, and licensees are distinct roles. No trademark is asserted for Cleopatra because a trademark-registry record was not reviewed.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official Masiá site calls Masiá Ciscar the central company of a shared group at the same address, but that statement is not a cap table and does not prove direct ownership of Masiá Breeding.

profiles.ownershipType: public-university professor and stone-fruit breeding leader
profiles.ownershipSummary: Rodrigo Arturo Infante Espiñeira is a University of Chile professor and breeder. Sweet Pekeetah is assigned to Universidad de Chile, while Andes Nec-6 is jointly assigned to Viveros Asociados Chile Limitada and Universidad de Chile. His earlier Universidad de La Frontera employment does not receive later University of Chile rights, and he is not the personal assignee.
profiles.headquarters: Faculty of Agricultural Sciences, Universidad de Chile, Santiago, Chile
profiles.leadershipSummary: Infante has worked full-time at Universidad de Chile since 1997 after a 1993-1997 Universidad de La Frontera role. The program operates with university researchers and commercial partners including Viveros Asociados/ANA Chile, creating an institutional and licensing bench beyond the individual breeder.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Split university-only Sweet Pekeetah from jointly assigned Andes records and preserve ANA/Viveros Asociados as commercial or co-owner roles only where the specific record supports them.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Norio Takada is a NARO pear breeder. His 2018-2020 assignment to the Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries Research Council was an employer posting, not a transfer of cultivar rights; NARO remains the institutional release and licensing counterparty for the pear portfolio.
profiles.headquarters: NARO Institute of Fruit Tree and Tea Science, Tsukuba, Ibaraki, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: The J-GLOBAL timeline places Takada back in NARO's Pear and Chestnut Breeding Unit from April 2026. A large co-breeder group including Sogo Nishio, Toshihiro Saito, Shingo Terakami, and Yukie Takeuchi provides institutional continuity.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Split the aggregated grape record from Takada's NARO pear identity unless independently proven. Do not assign historical NARO rights to the Research Council posting.
profiles.candidateParent: National Agriculture and Food Research Organization (NARO)
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The current unit affiliation and work history are supported, but the public researcher record does not provide a specific current job title for Takada.

profiles.ownershipType: former prefectural strawberry researcher and current industry-research institute officer
profiles.ownershipSummary: Toshiki Mori is the former Mie Prefecture Agricultural Research Institute breeder now identified as Secretary-General of the Seed-Propagating Strawberry Research Institute. Yotsuboshi is jointly held by NARO, Mie Prefecture, Kagawa Prefecture, and Chiba Prefecture; neither Mori personally nor his later institute should receive those rights. The holder for the recent Mienomori01 record remains unresolved.
profiles.headquarters: Mie Prefecture, Japan for the historical public breeding program; current Seed-Propagating Strawberry Research Institute headquarters unresolved
profiles.leadershipSummary: Yotsuboshi was created by four public institutions and commercialized through a broad national project. Mori's move to the seed-propagating institute is a visible operating transition, while later cultivars and multiple public partners demonstrate a bench beyond one individual.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Official contact for one Yotsuboshi co-holder

webResearchSources.label: BRAIN Mori role and Yotsuboshi history

profiles.webResearchNotes: Split Mie-only historical cultivars, jointly held Yotsuboshi, and Mienomori01. Keep Mienomori01 unresolved until an applicant or holder source is found; current employment is not evidence of ownership.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Haruhito Nakata is a named Ehime citrus breeder. Recent US plant patents including Mikanken15 are filed and assigned to Ehime Prefecture, and the records provide no personal rightsholder evidence.
profiles.headquarters: Ehime Prefecture Mikan Research Institute, Uwajima, Ehime, Japan; legal assignee Ehime Prefecture is headquartered in Matsuyama
profiles.leadershipSummary: The Mikanken program lists a 16-person inventor team spanning senior and newer breeders. This broad public-institution bench supports continuity and makes the profile non-actionable as an individual owner.

webResearchSources.label: Ehime Fruit Tree Research Center official overview

profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll the cited citrus rights into Ehime Prefecture, not Nakata personally. Preserve applications and grants by cultivar and jurisdiction.
profiles.candidateParent: Ehime Prefecture Mikan Research Institute / Fruit Tree Research Center
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The patent location, team context, and prefectural assignee support institutional affiliation, but the public sources reviewed do not state Nakata's current job title.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Yukinori Shigematsu is a named Ehime citrus breeder across early and current program generations. MAFF names Ehime Prefecture as holder of Ehime Kashi 28, and recent Mikanken US patents are assigned to Ehime Prefecture; no personal rightsholder evidence was found.
profiles.headquarters: Ehime Prefecture Mikan Research Institute, Uwajima, Ehime, Japan; legal assignee Ehime Prefecture is headquartered in Matsuyama
profiles.leadershipSummary: Shigematsu bridges the older Kanpei/Ehime Kashi line and the current 16-person Mikanken team. The continuing public breeding group supplies a strong institutional bench.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public-institution profile. Rights remain with Ehime Prefecture even where Shigematsu's breeding work predates the current research-center structure.
profiles.candidateParent: Ehime Prefecture Mikan Research Institute / Fruit Tree Research Center
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Repeated prefectural program inventorship and assignee records support affiliation, but the reviewed public sources do not state Shigematsu's current employment status or title.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Hiroko Hamada is a NARO citrus breeder. NARO publicly identifies Asuki as a NARO-developed cultivar, and the 2025 HAYASE patent is assigned to the National Agriculture and Food Research Organization; no personal rightsholder evidence was found.
profiles.headquarters: NARO citrus breeding program, Shizuoka, Japan; NARO corporate headquarters in Tsukuba, Ibaraki
profiles.leadershipSummary: Hamada appears in multi-person NARO citrus teams from the Asuki/Asumi work through the recent HAYASE filing. Senior and newer co-breeders provide institutional continuity.

webResearchSources.label: NARO technical report by Hiroko Hamada

profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll cited rights into NARO and preserve predecessor-organization names by filing date. The public affiliation is supported, but no individual title or personal contact is asserted.
profiles.candidateParent: NARO Institute of Fruit Tree and Tea Science
profiles.candidateParentBasis: NARO publications and patent records support the affiliation and Shizuoka citrus program, but the reviewed sources do not state Hamada's current job title.
